name: erc7730

metadata.name: erc7730
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
python-erc7730
==============

**This library provides tooling for the ERC-7730 standard.**

See documentation at <https://ledgerhq.github.io/python-erc7730>.

functions.name: erc7730.common.properties.has_property
documentation:
Check if the target has a property with the given name.

```
param target

object of dict like

param name

attribute name

return

true if the target has the property
```

functions.name: erc7730.common.properties.get_property
documentation:
Get the property with the given name on target object.

```
param target

object of dict like

param name

attribute name

return

value for property on target object
```

params.name: Argument(help="The files or directory paths to lint")
Argument(help="The files or directory paths to search")
Argument(help="The files or directory paths to search")
Option(help="Path to a JSON ABI file (to generate a calldata descriptor)")
Option(help="Path to an EIP-712 schema (to generate an EIP-712 descriptor)")
Option(help="The display name of the owner or target of the contract")
Option(help="The full legal name of the owner")
Option(help="URL with more info on the entity interacted with")

modules.name: erc7730.common.ledger

functions.name: erc7730.common.ledger.ledger_network_id
documentation: Get Ledger specific network id from chain id.

functions.name: erc7730.common.json.read_jsons_with_includes
documentation:
Read a list JSON files, recursively inlining any included files.

Keys from the calling file override those of the included file (the included file defines defaults).
Keys from the later files in the list override those of the first files.

Note:
  - circular includes are not detected and will result in a stack overflow.
  - "includes" key can only be used at root level of an object.

functions.name: erc7730.common.json.read_json_with_includes
documentation:
Read a JSON file, recursively inlining any included files.

Keys from the calling file override those of the included file (the included file defines defaults).

If include is a list, files are included in other they are defined, with later files overriding previous files.

Note:
  - circular includes are not detected and will result in a stack overflow.
  - "includes" key can only be used at root level of an object.

functions.name: erc7730.common.json._merge_dicts
documentation:
Merge d1 and d2, with priority to d2.

Recursively called when dicts are encountered.

This function assumes that if the same field is in both dicts, then the types must be the same.

Get supported chains from Etherscan.

```

Etherscan supported chains, with name/chain id/block explorer URL

EIP-155 chain ID

EVM contract address

raises NotImplementedError

if chain id not supported, API key not setup, or unexpected response

functions.name: erc7730.common.client.get
documentation:
Fetch data from a file or an HTTP URL and deserialize it.

This method implements some automated adaptations to handle user provided URLs:
: - GitHub: adaptation to "raw.githubusercontent.com"
  - Etherscan: rate limiting, API key parameter injection, "result" field unwrapping

```
param url

URL to get data from

param model

Pydantic model to deserialize the data

return

deserialized response

raises Exception

if URL type is not supported, API key not setup, or unexpected response
```

classes.name: erc7730.common.pydantic.ErrorTypeLabel
documentation:
Wrapper validator that replaces all errors with a simple message "expected a <type label>".

It is useful for annotating union types where pydantic returns multiple errors for each type it tries, or custom
base types such as pattern validated strings to get more user-friendly errors.

classes.name: erc7730.convert.ERC7730Converter

methods.name: erc7730.convert.ERC7730Converter.convert
documentation:
Convert a descriptor from/to ERC-7730.

Conversion may fail partially, in which case it should emit errors with WARNING level, or totally, in which case
it should emit errors with ERROR level.

Conversion can return a single descriptor, or multiple ones, in the form of a dictionary with unique
identifiers.

```
param descriptor

input descriptor to convert

param out

output sink

return

converted descriptor, or None if conversion failed
```

Converter from/to ERC-7730 descriptor.

A converter may fail partially, in which case it should emit errors with ERROR level, or totally, in which case it
should emit errors with FATAL level.

functions.name: erc7730.list.list.list_all
documentation:
List all ERC-7730 descriptor files at given paths.

Paths can be files or directories, in which case all descriptor files in the directory are recursively listed.

```

paths to search for descriptor files

return

```

paths to search for descriptor files

param out

error handler
```

functions.name: erc7730.lint.lint.lint_all
documentation:
Lint all ERC-7730 descriptor files at given paths.

Paths can be files or directories, in which case all JSON files in the directory are recursively linted.

paths to apply linter on

param out

output adder

return

```

converter to use

classes.name: erc7730.lint.lint_validate_abi.ValidateABILinter
documentation:
- resolves the ABI from the descriptor (URL or provided)
- resolves the ABI from *scan (given chainId and address of descriptor)
- => compares the two ABIs

classes.name: erc7730.lint.ERC7730Linter
documentation:
Linter for ERC-7730 descriptors, inspects a (structurally valid) descriptor and emits notes, warnings, or
errors.

A linter may emit false positives or false negatives. It is up to the user to interpret the output.

classes.name: erc7730.lint.lint_transaction_type_classifier.ClassifyTransactionTypeLinter
documentation:
- given schema/ABI, classify the transaction type
- if class found, check descriptor display fields against predefined ruleset
- possible class (swap, staking withdraw, staking deposit)

classes.name: erc7730.lint.lint_transaction_type_classifier.DisplayFormatChecker

methods.name: erc7730.lint.lint_transaction_type_classifier.DisplayFormatChecker._fields_contain
documentation: Check if the provided keyword is contained in one of the fields (case insensitive)

Given a transaction class and a display formats, check if all the required fields of a given
transaction class are being displayed.
If a field is missing emit an error.

classes.name: erc7730.convert.resolved.convert_erc7730_input_to_resolved.ERC7730InputToResolved
documentation:
Converts ERC-7730 descriptor input to resolved form.

After conversion, the descriptor is in resolved form:
 - URLs have been fetched
 - Contract addresses have been normalized to lowercase
 - References have been inlined
 - Constants have been inlined
 - Field definitions have been inlined
 - Nested fields have been flattened where possible
 - Selectors have been converted to 4 bytes form

classes.name: erc7730.lint.lint_base.MultiLinter
documentation: A linter that runs multiple linters in sequence.

classes.name: erc7730.lint.lint_validate_display_fields.ValidateDisplayFieldsLinter
documentation:
- for each field of schema/ABI, check that there is a display field
- for each field, check that display configuration is relevant with field type

classes.name: erc7730.model.paths.Field
documentation: A path component designating a field in a structured data schema.

classes.name: erc7730.model.paths.ArrayElement
documentation: A path component designating a single element of an array.

classes.name: erc7730.model.paths.ArraySlice
documentation: A path component designating an element range of an array (in which case, the path targets multiple values).

classes.name: erc7730.model.paths.Array
documentation: A path component designating all elements of an array (in which case, the path targets multiple values).

classes.name: erc7730.model.paths.ContainerField

class_variables.name: erc7730.model.paths.ContainerField.VALUE
documentation: The native currency value of the transaction containing the structured data.

class_variables.name: erc7730.model.paths.ContainerField.FROM
documentation: The address of the sender of the transaction / signer of the message.

class_variables.name: erc7730.model.paths.ContainerField.TO
documentation: The destination address of the containing transaction, ie the target smart contract address.

Path applying to the container of the structured data to be signed.

Such paths are prefixed with "@".

classes.name: erc7730.model.paths.ContainerPath
documentation:
Path applying to the container of the structured data to be signed.

Such paths are prefixed with "@".

classes.name: erc7730.model.paths.DataPath
documentation:
Path applying to the structured data schema (ABI path for contracts, path in the message types itself for
EIP-712).

A data path can reference multiple values if it contains array elements or slices.

Such paths are prefixed with "#".

classes.name: erc7730.model.paths.DescriptorPath
documentation:
Path applying to the current file describing the structured data formatting, after merging with includes.

A descriptor path can only reference a single value in the document.

Such paths are prefixed with "$".

functions.name: erc7730.generate.generate.generate_descriptor
documentation:
Generate an ERC-7730 descriptor.

If an EIP-712 schema file is provided, an EIP-712 descriptor is generated for this schema, otherwise a calldata
descriptor. If no ABI file is supplied, the ABIs are fetched from Etherscan using the chain id / contract address.

```
param chain_id

contract chain id

param contract_address

contract address

param abi_file

path to a JSON ABI file (to generate a calldata descriptor)

param eip712_schema_file

path to an EIP-712 schema (to generate an EIP-712 descriptor)

param owner

the display name of the owner or target of the contract / message to be clear signed

param legal_name

the full legal name of the owner if different from the owner field

param url

URL with more info on the entity the user interacts with

return

a generated ERC-7730 descriptor
```

functions.name: erc7730.generate.schema_tree.eip712_schema_to_tree
documentation:
Convert an EIP-712 schema to a schema tree.

A schema tree is a tree representation of the EIP-712 schema, enriched with some metadata to ease crafting
paths to access values in the message.

```
param schema

EIP-712 schema

return

Schema tree
```

functions.name: erc7730.generate.schema_tree._eip712_struct_type_to_tree
documentation:
Convert a complex EIP-712 type to a schema tree node (can be the primary type directly).

```
param fields

type fields

param types

all schema types

return

Schema tree
```

functions.name: erc7730.generate.schema_tree._eip712_field_to_tree
documentation:
Convert an EIP-712 type to a schema tree node.

```
param field

ABI element (can be a single component, or a function input)

param types

all schema types

return

Schema tree
```

functions.name: erc7730.generate.schema_tree.abi_function_to_tree
documentation:
Convert a function ABI to a schema tree.

A schema tree is a tree representation of the ABI of a function inputs, enriched with some metadata to ease
crafting paths to access values in the serialized calldata.

```
param function

function ABI

return

Schema tree
```

functions.name: erc7730.generate.schema_tree._abi_struct_component_to_tree
documentation:
Convert a struct-like ABI component to a schema tree node (can be the top level function directly).

```
param inp

ABI element

return

Schema tree
```

functions.name: erc7730.generate.schema_tree._abi_component_to_tree
documentation:
Convert an ABI component to a schema tree node.

```
param inp

ABI element (can be a single component, or a function input)

return

Schema tree
```

classes.name: erc7730.model.paths.path_parser.PathTransformer

modules.name: erc7730.model.metadata
documentation:
Object model for ERC-7730 descriptors `metadata` section.

Specification: https://github.com/LedgerHQ/clear-signing-erc7730-registry/tree/master/specs
JSON schema: https://github.com/LedgerHQ/clear-signing-erc7730-registry/blob/master/specs/erc7730-v1.schema.json

classes.name: erc7730.model.metadata.OwnerInfo
documentation:
Main contract's owner detailed information.

The owner info section contains detailed information about the owner or target of the contract / message to be
clear signed.
ruff: noqa: N815 - camel case field names are tolerated to match schema

classes.name: erc7730.model.metadata.TokenInfo
documentation:
Token Description.

A description of an ERC20 token exported by this format, that should be trusted. Not mandatory if the
corresponding metadata can be fetched from the contract itself.

classes.name: erc7730.model.metadata.Metadata
documentation:
Metadata Section.

The metadata section contains information about constant values relevant in the scope of the current contract /
message (as matched by the `context` section)

modules.name: erc7730.model.input.descriptor
documentation:
Package implementing an object model for ERC-7730 input descriptors.

This model represents descriptors before resolution phase:
    - URLs have been not been fetched yet
    - Contract addresses have not been normalized to lowercase
    - References have not been inlined
    - Constants have not been inlined
    - Field definitions have not been inlined
    - Nested fields have been flattened where possible
    - Selectors have been converted to 4 bytes form

classes.name: erc7730.model.input.descriptor.InputERC7730Descriptor
documentation:
An ERC7730 Clear Signing descriptor.

This model is directly serializable back the original JSON document.

Specification: https://github.com/LedgerHQ/clear-signing-erc7730-registry/tree/master/specs

JSON schema: https://github.com/LedgerHQ/clear-signing-erc7730-registry/blob/master/specs/erc7730-v1.schema.json
ruff: noqa: N815 - camel case field names are tolerated to match schema

modules.name: erc7730.model.input
documentation:
Package implementing an object model for ERC-7730 input descriptors.

This model is directly serializable back the original JSON document.

modules.name: erc7730.model.input.metadata
documentation:
Object model for ERC-7730 descriptors `metadata` section.

Specification: https://github.com/LedgerHQ/clear-signing-erc7730-registry/tree/master/specs
JSON schema: https://github.com/LedgerHQ/clear-signing-erc7730-registry/blob/master/specs/erc7730-v1.schema.json

classes.name: erc7730.model.input.metadata.InputMetadata
documentation:
Metadata Section.

The metadata section contains information about constant values relevant in the scope of the current contract /
message (as matched by the `context` section)
ruff: noqa: N815 - camel case field names are tolerated to match schema

classes.name: erc7730.model.input.context.InputDomain
documentation:
EIP 712 Domain Binding constraint.

Each value of the domain constraint MUST match the corresponding eip 712 message domain value.
ruff: noqa: N815 - camel case field names are tolerated to match schema

classes.name: erc7730.model.input.context.InputDeployment
documentation:
A deployment describing where the contract is deployed.

The target contract (Tx to or factory) MUST match one of those deployments.

classes.name: erc7730.model.input.context.InputFactory
documentation: A factory constraint is used to check whether the target contract is deployed by a specified factory.

classes.name: erc7730.model.input.context.InputContract
documentation:
The contract binding context is a set constraints that are used to bind the ERC7730 file to a specific smart
contract.

classes.name: erc7730.model.input.context.InputEIP712
documentation:
EIP 712 Binding.

The EIP-712 binding context is a set of constraints that must be verified by the message being signed.

classes.name: erc7730.model.input.context.InputContractContext
documentation:
Contract Binding Context.

The contract binding context is a set constraints that are used to bind the ERC7730 file to a specific smart
contract.

classes.name: erc7730.model.input.context.InputEIP712Context
documentation:
EIP 712 Binding.

The EIP-712 binding context is a set of constraints that must be verified by the message being signed.

modules.name: erc7730.model.input.lenses
documentation: Utilities for accessing ERC-7730 input descriptors nested fields.

functions.name: erc7730.model.input.lenses.get_chain_ids
documentation: Get deployment chaind ids for a descriptor.

functions.name: erc7730.model.input.lenses.get_deployments
documentation: Get deployments section for a descriptor.

functions.name: erc7730.model.input.lenses.get_binding_context
documentation: Get binding context for a descriptor.

modules.name: erc7730.model
documentation: Package implementing an object model for ERC-7730 descriptors.

classes.name: erc7730.model.ERC7730ModelType
documentation: The type of the ERC-7730 model.

modules.name: erc7730.model.resolved.descriptor
documentation:
Module implementing an object model for ERC-7730 resolved descriptors.

This model represents descriptors after resolution phase:
    - URLs have been fetched
    - Contract addresses have been normalized to lowercase
    - References have been inlined
    - Constants have been inlined
    - Field definitions have been inlined
    - Nested fields have been flattened where possible
    - Selectors have been converted to 4 bytes form

classes.name: erc7730.model.resolved.descriptor.ResolvedERC7730Descriptor
documentation:
An ERC7730 Clear Signing descriptor.

This model represents descriptors after resolution phase:
    - URLs have been fetched
    - Contract addresses have been normalized to lowercase
    - References have been inlined
    - Constants have been inlined
    - Field definitions have been inlined
    - Nested fields have been flattened where possible
    - Selectors have been converted to 4 bytes form

Specification: https://github.com/LedgerHQ/clear-signing-erc7730-registry/tree/master/specs

JSON schema: https://github.com/LedgerHQ/clear-signing-erc7730-registry/blob/master/specs/erc7730-v1.schema.json
ruff: noqa: N815 - camel case field names are tolerated to match schema

modules.name: erc7730.model.resolved
documentation:
Package implementing an object model for ERC-7730 resolved descriptors.

This model represents descriptors after resolution phase:
    - URLs have been fetched
    - Contract addresses have been normalized to lowercase
    - References have been inlined
    - Constants have been inlined
    - Field definitions have been inlined
    - Nested fields have been flattened where possible
    - Selectors have been converted to 4 bytes form

classes.name: erc7730.model.input.display.InputFieldBase
documentation: A field formatter, containing formatting information of a single field in a message.
ruff: noqa: N815 - camel case field names are tolerated to match schema

classes.name: erc7730.model.input.display.InputReference
documentation:
A reference to a shared definition that should be used as the field formatting definition.

The value is the key in the display definitions section, as a path expression $.display.definitions.DEFINITION_NAME.
It is used to share definitions between multiple messages / functions.

classes.name: erc7730.model.input.display.InputNestedFields
documentation:
A single set of field formats, allowing recursivity in the schema.

Used to group whole definitions for structures for instance. This allows nesting definitions of formats, but note
that support for deep nesting will be device dependent.

functions.name: erc7730.model.paths.path_ops.descriptor_path_strip_prefix
documentation:
Strip expected prefix from a descriptor path, raising an error if the prefix is not matching.

```
param path

path to strip

param prefix

prefix to strip

return

path without prefix

raises ValueError

if the path does not start with the prefix
```

functions.name: erc7730.model.paths.path_ops.data_path_strip_prefix
documentation:
Strip expected prefix from a data path, raising an error if the prefix is not matching.

```
param path

path to strip

param prefix

prefix to strip

return

path without prefix

raises ValueError

if the path does not start with the prefix
```

documentation:

modules.name: erc7730.model.abi
documentation:
Object model for Solidity ABIs.

See https://docs.soliditylang.org/en/latest/abi-spec.html

classes.name: erc7730.model.abi.StateMutability
documentation: ruff: noqa: N815 - camel case field names are tolerated to match schema

classes.name: erc7730.model.display.FieldFormat

class_variables.name: erc7730.model.display.FieldFormat.RAW
documentation: The field should be displayed as the natural representation of the underlying structured data type.

class_variables.name: erc7730.model.display.FieldFormat.ADDRESS_NAME
documentation:
The field should be displayed as a trusted name, or as a raw address if no names are found in trusted sources.
List of trusted sources can be optionally specified in parameters.

class_variables.name: erc7730.model.display.FieldFormat.CALL_DATA
documentation:
The field is itself a calldata embedded in main call. Another ERC 7730 should be used to parse this field. If not
available or not supported, the wallet MAY display a hash of the embedded calldata instead.

class_variables.name: erc7730.model.display.FieldFormat.AMOUNT
documentation:
The field should be displayed as an amount in underlying currency, converted using the best magnitude / ticker
available.

class_variables.name: erc7730.model.display.FieldFormat.TOKEN_AMOUNT
documentation:
The field should be displayed as an amount, preceded by the ticker. The magnitude and ticker should be derived
from the tokenPath parameter corresponding metadata.
nosec B105 - bandit false positive

class_variables.name: erc7730.model.display.FieldFormat.NFT_NAME
documentation:
The field should be displayed as a single NFT names, or as a raw token Id if a specific name is not found.
Collection is specified by the collectionPath parameter.

class_variables.name: erc7730.model.display.FieldFormat.DATE
documentation:
The field should be displayed as a date. Suggested RFC3339 representation. Parameter specifies the encoding of
the date.

class_variables.name: erc7730.model.display.FieldFormat.DURATION
documentation: The field should be displayed as a duration in HH:MM:ss form. Value is interpreted as a number of seconds.

class_variables.name: erc7730.model.display.FieldFormat.UNIT
documentation:
The field should be displayed as a percentage. Magnitude of the percentage encoding is specified as a parameter.
Example: a value of 3000 with magnitude 4 is displayed as 0.3%.

class_variables.name: erc7730.model.display.FieldFormat.ENUM
documentation:
The field should be displayed as a human readable string by converting the value using the enum referenced in
parameters.
The format of the field, that will be used to format the field value in a human readable way.
ruff: noqa: N815 - camel case field names are tolerated to match schema

classes.name: erc7730.model.display.DateEncoding

class_variables.name: erc7730.model.display.DateEncoding.BLOCKHEIGHT
documentation: The date is encoded as a block height.

class_variables.name: erc7730.model.display.DateEncoding.TIMESTAMP
documentation: The date is encoded as a timestamp.
The encoding for a date.

classes.name: erc7730.model.display.AddressNameType

class_variables.name: erc7730.model.display.AddressNameType.WALLET
documentation: Address is an account controlled by the wallet.

class_variables.name: erc7730.model.display.AddressNameType.EOA
documentation: Address is an Externally Owned Account.

class_variables.name: erc7730.model.display.AddressNameType.CONTRACT
documentation: Address is a well known smartcontract.

class_variables.name: erc7730.model.display.AddressNameType.TOKEN
documentation: Address is a well known ERC-20 token.
nosec B105 - bandit false positive

class_variables.name: erc7730.model.display.AddressNameType.COLLECTION
documentation: Address is a well known NFT collection.
The type of address to display. Restrict allowable sources of names and MAY lead to additional checks from wallets.

classes.name: erc7730.model.display.Screen
documentation:
Screens section is used to group multiple fields to display into screens. Each key is a wallet type name. The
format of the screens is wallet type dependent, as well as what can be done (reordering fields, max number of
screens, etc...). See each wallet manufacturer documentation for more information.

classes.name: erc7730.model.display.FormatBase
documentation: A structured data format specification, containing formatting information of fields in a single type of message.

modules.name: erc7730.model.base
documentation:
Base model for library, using pydantic.

See https://docs.pydantic.dev

classes.name: erc7730.model.base.Model

methods.name: erc7730.model.base.Model.load
documentation:
Load a model from a JSON file.

```
param path

file path

return

validated in-memory representation of model

raises Exception

if the file does not exist or has validation errors
```

methods.name: erc7730.model.base.Model.load_or_none
documentation:
Load a model from a JSON file.

```
param path

file path

return

validated in-memory representation of descriptor, or None if file does not exist

raises Exception

if the file has validation errors
```

methods.name: erc7730.model.base.Model.save
documentation: Write a model to a JSON file, creating parent directories as needed.

methods.name: erc7730.model.base.Model.to_json_string
documentation:
Serialize the model to a JSON string.

```
return

JSON representation of model, serialized as a string
```

Base model for library, using pydantic.

See https://docs.pydantic.dev

modules.name: erc7730.format
documentation: Package implementing formatting commands to normalize descriptor files.

functions.name: erc7730.model.unions.field_discriminator
documentation:
Discriminator function for the Field union type.

```
param v

deserialized raw data

return

the discriminator tag
```

```

return

functions.name: erc7730.format.format.format_all_and_print_errors
documentation:
Format all ERC-7730 descriptor files at given paths and print errors.

```
param paths

paths to apply formatter on

return

true if not errors occurred
```

functions.name: erc7730.format.format.format_all
documentation:
Format all ERC-7730 descriptor files at given paths.

Paths can be files or directories, in which case all descriptor files in the directory are recursively formatted.

```
param paths

paths to apply formatter on

param out

output adder

return

number of files formatted
```

functions.name: erc7730.format.format.format_file
documentation:
Format a single ERC-7730 descriptor file.

```
param path

ERC-7730 descriptor file path

param show_as

if provided, print this label instead of the file path

param out

error handler
```
